title: Maxillaria cochleata
taxonomy: Maxillaria cochleata is a species of orchid that belongs to the family Orchidaceae. The genus Maxillaria is a large and diverse group of orchids, with over 600 species. Maxillaria cochleata is a terrestrial orchid that is native to Central and South America. It is a type of epiphyte, which means it grows on other plants, often in a humid and shaded environment. Within the genus Maxillaria, M. cochleata is classified as a species of the subtribe Maxillariinae.

The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as wounds, infections, and respiratory issues.
